decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.27°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. The most effective primary lever is to increase the heat spreader conductivity to enhance lateral heat spreading and reduce thermal resistance. I will apply a conservative 1.5x increase to the spreader material conductivity, staying well within the allowed 2.0x step ratio.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity improves lateral heat distribution away from the hot spot, directly lowering the peak chip temperature.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards or below the 85.0°C constraint.
More uniform temperature profile across the heat spreader layer.
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate or boundary conditions rather than the spreader itself.
High conductivity values may require finer mesh resolution in future steps to maintain numerical accuracy, though current mesh should suffice for this increment.
